description_long: This is a classic cylindrical fire hydrant with a vertically ribbed red body and a domed metallic cap on top. The hydrant features two prominent hose connection outlets on opposite sides, each with a hexagonal nut for attaching hoses. The cap and base are metallic gray, contrasting with the weathered red paint of the main body, which shows signs of wear and exposure. The hydrant's structure is robust, designed to withstand high water pressure and external impacts. No chains or manufacturer markings are visible on the exterior. The overall appearance suggests it is intended for installation along urban streets or sidewalks for emergency fire-fighting use.
description: A red, ribbed cylindrical fire hydrant with a metallic domed cap and two hose outlets, showing signs of wear and urban use.
description_view_0: Side view showing the ribbed red body and the metallic cap, with one hose outlet visible protruding to the right.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the front hose outlet with a hexagonal nut and the metallic cap above.
description_view_2: Direct view of the main hose outlet and cap, with the ribbed body and both outlets partially visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the ribbed body and the second hose outlet, with the cap and base in clear view.
